summary: Frontend-only verification of Socket.IO real-time unread badge updates (notifications + messages) replacing the 30s polling loop. All 6 test scenarios passed end-to-end on the preview URL.

T1_realtime_notification_badge.detail: Triggered POST /api/notifications/create/like as demo3 -> demo1. Bell badge in demo1's open tab updated from (none) to '1' within ~0.6s without page refresh. Verified via lucide-bell button span in header.

T2_mark_all_read_pushes_zero.detail: PUT /api/notifications/mark-all-read (Bearer demo1 JWT) -> bell badge cleared within ~0.6s in the open tab.

T3_realtime_message_badge.detail: Created conversation + sent message as demo3 -> demo1. inbox-dropdown-btn badge updated to '1' within ~0.6s without refresh.

T4_no_polling_regression.detail: Monitored network for 45s on /victory-lane after login. /api/notifications/unread-count called 1x (initial), /api/messages/unread-count called 4x but ALL clustered in first 0.3s of mount (likely React StrictMode + initial layout double-mount of InboxDropdown). No 30-second interval pattern detected. No polling regression.

T5_victory_lane_regression.detail: /victory-lane loads with Live feed text, [Social Socket] Connected/Authenticated logs present, no console errors.

T6_messages_page_regression.detail: /messages page loads cleanly, content rendered, no console errors.

minor_observations: InboxDropdown triggers 4 initial /api/messages/unread-count calls within the first 300ms after login redirect (not polling - they are clustered, not interval-spaced). Likely React 18 StrictMode double-mount + layout re-mount when navigating from /login to /victory-lane. Could optionally be deduped, but does NOT affect the 'no polling regression' requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: NotificationContext.js line 6 uses window.location.origin instead of process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (works in preview because origin == backend URL, but inconsistent with the project convention used in InboxDropdown.js). Minor.
useSocialSocket.js also uses window.location.origin (same minor inconsistency).
Both files correctly use the singleton pattern so the socket is kept alive across route changes — good.
test_credentials: demo1/demo1 and demo3/demo3 via POST /api/auth/demo-login (UI works with same).
seed_data_creation: Created 1 like notification + 1 conversation message between demo3 and demo1 during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Real-time unread push is fully working. The social socket (path /api/socialws/socket.io/) and messaging socket (path /api/messagews/socket.io/) both emit unread_*_count events on connect, on create, and on mark-read. Frontend listeners in NotificationContext.js and InboxDropdown.js update badges within sub-second. No 30s polling fallback observed.
